name: MiniMax-M2
description: MiniMax's MIT-licensed 230B MoE with only 10B active parameters, optimized for agentic tool calling and coding. Topped open-model agentic rankings at launch and undercut Claude pricing by roughly 92% while remaining fast due to its small active footprint.

evidence.value: Strong SWE-bench and Terminal-Bench results for an open model at launch
Ranked the leading open-source model for agentic coding workflows at launch

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Vendor benchmarks corroborated by independent press and leaderboard coverage; superseded at the top by 2026 releases

evidence.value: Competitive reasoning for its 10B-active footprint; interleaved thinking format

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Vendor-reported reasoning benchmarks and community evaluation

evidence.value: Highest composite intelligence score among open-weight models at launch window

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Independent composite benchmarking across knowledge domains

evidence.value: Stable tool-calling behavior across long agent loops

output_consistency.methodology: Community testing of repeated runs and agentic trajectories

evidence.value: Fast responses — 10B active parameters yield roughly 2x the speed of comparable dense models

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 ~4.0s across diverse workloads

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: ~205K token context window
First-party API generally stable; open weights enable self-hosted redundancy

uptime.methodology: Review of platform availability and self-hosting fallback options

performance_reliability.notes: Was the leading open agentic model at its October 2025 launch; still strong, but 2026 releases (GLM-5, Kimi K2.6) have surpassed it on raw benchmarks. Its 10B-active design remains a standout for speed and serving cost. Successor MiniMax-M3 (428B total / 23B active, 1M context, native multimodality) launched 2026-06-01 with open weights published on Hugging Face by 2026-06-07.

evidence.value: Safety tuning described; no published third-party prompt-injection audit

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Review of vendor documentation and community testing against OWASP LLM01 patterns

evidence.value: Standard alignment tuning; open weights allow guardrail removal in derivatives

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Testing against adversarial prompt datasets; deployer-dependent for self-hosted use

evidence.value: Standard data handling on first-party API; full control when self-hosted

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and self-hosting data-control options

evidence.value: Safety post-training applied; refusal behavior in line with peer open models

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting; OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible endpoints

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Standard open-model posture without third-party audits. Self-hosting shifts security responsibility to the deployer.

data_residency.value: China (first-party MiniMax API); any jurisdiction when self-hosted or via Western hosts

evidence.value: MiniMax is a China-based provider; first-party API data processed under Chinese jurisdiction
MIT weights served by Western inference providers, enabling non-China residency

data_residency.methodology: Review of provider jurisdiction and third-party hosting options

evidence.value: Standard API data terms; self-hosting removes the concern entirely

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

data_retention.value: Per MiniMax policy on first-party API (China jurisdiction); zero when self-hosted

evidence.value: First-party retention governed by Chinese data regulations; self-hosted deployments retain nothing externally

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and deployment-dependent retention

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; no managed PII tooling

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: No published SOC 2 / HIPAA / GDPR attestations for the first-party API

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: MIT-licensed self-hosting gives complete data control and zero external retention; 10B active makes this unusually affordable

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of self-hosting deployment options enabling zero retention

privacy_compliance.notes: First-party MiniMax API operates under Chinese jurisdiction — a material caveat for Western regulated industries. The small 10B-active footprint makes self-hosted mitigation cheaper than for other frontier-scale open models.

evidence.value: Interleaved thinking format exposes reasoning between tool calls, aiding agent-loop auditability

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and trajectory inspectability

evidence.value: Moderate hallucination rate; tool-grounded workflows perform better than closed-book QA

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on factual QA datasets and tool-augmented workflows

evidence.value: Limited published bias evaluation

bias_fairness.methodology: Review of published bias benchmarks and community evaluations

evidence.value: Basic uncertainty expression; no calibrated confidence outputs

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Clear documentation of 230B/10B MoE architecture, MIT license, benchmarks, and deployment guidance

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: Architecture documented; training data composition not disclosed in detail

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Built-in safety tuning; deployers of open weights must layer their own guardrails

trust_transparency.notes: Open weights and interleaved-thinking traces provide reasonable transparency; training data disclosure and formal bias/safety evaluations are limited.

evidence.value: OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible endpoints with streaming and tool calling, easing migration

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Compatibility with mainstream OpenAI/Anthropic SDKs; first-party tooling adequate

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Successor M3 (428B/23B, 1M context, native multimodal) launched 2026-06-01; open weights published on Hugging Face by 2026-06-07, though training code and inference operators were not released. M2 weights remain available

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning practices and weight availability across releases

evidence.value: Basic usage dashboard; self-hosted observability is deployer-built

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: GitHub and community support; limited English-language enterprise support

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: vLLM/SGLang support, OpenRouter availability, popular in open-source agent frameworks

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party hosting, integrations, and tooling

evidence.value: MIT license per model card, unrestricted commercial use and derivatives

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Clean MIT licensing and dual OpenAI/Anthropic API compatibility lower switching costs. Successor M3's weights shipped 2026-06-07, resolving the earlier roadmap uncertainty; M2 remains available but is now the previous generation.

code-generation.notes: Strong agentic coding at exceptional cost-efficiency; no longer the open-weight leader after 2026 releases.

customer-support.notes: Fast (10B active) and very cheap — well suited to high-volume conversational workloads.

content-creation.notes: Adequate generation quality at minimal cost.

data-analysis.notes: Good tool-calling for analysis pipelines; weaker raw reasoning than GLM-5 or Kimi K2.6.

research-assistant.notes: Strong agentic search and tool orchestration; 205K context handles long documents.

legal-compliance.notes: China-jurisdiction first-party API and absent Western certifications are blockers unless self-hosted.

healthcare.notes: Not recommended via first-party API; self-hosted deployment in a compliant environment is the only viable path.

financial-analysis.notes: Capable and cheap; data residency requires self-hosting for regulated firms.

education.notes: Good tutoring at very low cost; well suited to high-volume educational platforms.

creative-writing.notes: Serviceable creative output; not its design focus.

strengths: Topped open-model agentic tool-calling rankings at launch (October 2025)
Exceptional efficiency: 10B active of 230B total — fast inference and cheap self-hosting
Launched at roughly 8% of Claude pricing, among the best cost/capability ratios available
Clean MIT license with full self-hosting rights
OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible APIs minimize migration effort
~205K context window for long-document and long-trajectory work
limitations: First-party MiniMax API processes data under Chinese jurisdiction with no published Western compliance certifications
Surpassed on raw benchmarks by 2026 open-weight releases (GLM-5, Kimi K2.6)
Superseded within MiniMax's own lineup: M3 (1M context, native multimodality) shipped with open weights in June 2026
Text-only — no vision or audio modalities
Limited published bias, safety, and red-team evaluations
Interleaved thinking format requires prompt-handling care in some frameworks
best_for: High-volume agentic tool-calling workloads where cost per trajectory dominates
Teams self-hosting on modest GPU budgets (10B active footprint)
Drop-in replacement experiments via OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible APIs
Latency-sensitive agent loops needing fast token generation
not_recommended_for: Regulated Western workloads (healthcare, legal, finance) on the first-party API
Teams needing the absolute strongest open-weight benchmark performance in mid-2026
Multimodal applications requiring image or audio input

pricing.notes: Launched at roughly 8% of Claude Sonnet pricing; third-party host pricing varies. First-party rates unchanged in July 2026 checks.

metadata.architecture: Mixture-of-Experts: 230B total / 10B active parameters, interleaved thinking for agentic tool use
